commands.oneClickInstall: claude plugin marketplace add wunderlabs-dev/claudebin && claude plugin install claudebin@claudebin-marketplace

cliAuth.title:
<gradient>CLI</gradient>
Authentication
cliAuth.description: Securely connect your terminal to Claudebin
cliAuth.successDescription: Your terminal is now connected to Claudebin. You can close this window and return to your terminal.
cliAuth.invalidLinkDescription: This authentication link is missing the required code. Please use the link provided in your terminal.
cliAuth.invalidCodeDescription: This authentication code doesn't exist or has already been used. Please run the auth command again.
cliAuth.expiredDescription: This authentication code has expired. Please run the auth command again to get a new link.
cliAuth.failedDescription: Something went wrong while connecting your terminal. Please try again.

overview.title: 1. Overview
overview.p1: Claudebin is a free, open-source web application that allows users to publish and share Claude Code session logs as browsable and embeddable web pages.
overview.item5: Your rights under GDPR and CCPA
overview.p3: If you use Claudebin, you are trusting us with data. Here's exactly what that means.
overview.p4: Claudebin is an independent project, not affiliated with Anthropic.

auth.title: A. Authentication Data (Google and GitHub OAuth)
auth.p1: Users authenticate via Google OAuth or GitHub OAuth.
auth.item1: Provider username or generated Claudebin username
auth.p3: Email addresses are collected by the authentication provider but are not stored in or exposed through public-facing tables within Claudebin.
auth.p4: We do not support email/password login.

content.title: B. User-Published Content
content.p1: When you publish a session, we store the full Claude Code session, including:
content.item3: Tool call results (file reads, writes, bash commands)
content.p3: Raw session data (JSONL format, up to 50MB) is stored in Supabase Storage.
content.p4: If you publish it, assume it may be visible indefinitely.

profile.title: C. Public Profile Information
profile.p3: If a session is public, its contents are public.

views.title: D. First-Party View Counting
views.p1: We track page views using a 1x1 transparent pixel.
views.p2: To reduce abuse and count unique views:
views.item1: IP address and User-Agent are hashed (SHA-256, truncated to 16 characters)
views.item2: Raw IP addresses and User-Agents are never stored
views.item4: Records older than 30 days are automatically deleted
views.p3: This system is privacy-preserving by design.

analytics.title: E. Analytics & Monitoring
analytics.p1: We use the following third-party services:
analytics.item1: Vercel Analytics — page-view analytics (loaded on all pages)
analytics.item2: Vercel Speed Insights — performance telemetry (loaded on all pages)
analytics.item3: Google Analytics — optional; only loaded if configured
analytics.item4: Sentry — error monitoring and performance tracing (production only, routed through /monitoring tunnel; captures errors and ~10% of transactions)
analytics.p2: These services may process device data, usage data, and performance metrics.

ai.title: F. AI-Generated Session Titles
ai.item1: The first 2000 characters of user prompts are sent server-side to OpenRouter
ai.item2: OpenRouter routes the request to OpenAI's GPT-4o-mini
ai.item3: A session title is generated automatically
ai.p2: This means a portion of your prompt content is transmitted to a third-party AI provider solely for title generation.

cookies.title: G. Cookies
cookies.p1: We use authentication session cookies managed by Supabase SSR:
cookies.p2: We do not set additional custom tracking cookies.
cookies.p3: However, third-party analytics providers may use their own cookies depending on configuration.

emails.title: H. Emails Sent to Us
emails.p1: All emails received by Wunderlabs, including communications sent to office@wunderlabs.dev, are the property of Wunderlabs and may be stored, archived, and processed for operational, legal, or administrative purposes.

infrastructure.title: 3. Infrastructure & Hosting Providers
infrastructure.p2: These providers may process data on our behalf under their respective privacy policies and data processing agreements.

legal.title: 4. Legal Bases for Processing (GDPR)
legal.p1: For users in the European Economic Area (EEA), processing is based on:
legal.item1: Performance of a contract — providing the service
legal.item2: Legitimate interests — security, abuse prevention, analytics
legal.item3: Consent — where required for optional analytics
legal.item4: Legal obligations — compliance with applicable laws

retention.title: 5. Data Retention
retention.item1: Published sessions: retained indefinitely unless manually removed
retention.item2: View records: automatically deleted after 30 days
retention.item3: Error logs: retained per Sentry retention policies
retention.item4: Authentication data: retained while account exists
retention.item5: Soft-deleted accounts: profile hidden, data remains stored
retention.p1: We retain data only as long as necessary to operate the service and comply with legal obligations.

deletion.title: 6. Account Deletion
deletion.p1: Account deletion is a soft delete:
deletion.item1: Your profile becomes hidden from the UI
deletion.item2: Your data remains in the database

rights.title: 7. Your Rights (GDPR & CCPA)
rights.p1: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the right to:
rights.item7: Request disclosure of categories of data collected (CCPA)
rights.p2: We do not sell personal information.
rights.p4: We may require identity verification before fulfilling requests.

transfers.title: 8. International Data Transfers
transfers.p1: Your data may be processed in countries outside your jurisdiction, including the United States. We rely on appropriate safeguards, including contractual protections, where required.

security.title: 9. Security
security.p1: We implement technical and organizational measures to protect data, including:
security.p2: No system is perfectly secure. If you publish sensitive information in a public session, it becomes public.

children.title: 10. Children's Privacy
children.p1: Claudebin is not intended for children under 13.
children.p2: We do not knowingly collect data from children under 13. If you believe a child has provided personal information, contact us and we will take appropriate action.

payments.title: 11. Payments
payments.p2: We do not collect payment, billing, or subscription data.

changes.title: 12. Changes to This Policy
changes.p1: We may update this Privacy Policy periodically. Continued use of the service after changes means you accept the updated policy.

contactInfo.title: 13. Contact
contactInfo.p3: If you have privacy concerns, data requests, or security questions, contact us directly.

footer.copyright: © {year} Claudebin. A <link>Wunderlabs</link> project. All rights reserved.
footer.disclaimer: Independent project, not affiliated with Anthropic.
